destinations.evidence: Scheduled hashing uses the dedicated signing-field encoding and excludes runtime metadata.
destinations.snippet: fn tx_hash_with_type(...) { buf.put_u8(ty); self.rlp_encode_fields_for_signing(&mut buf); keccak256(&buf) }

destinations.evidence: Async commitment has an analogous dedicated signing-payload path.

rationale: Multiple candidates preserve the scheduled special hash path.
statement: For scheduled transactions, TransactionSigned hash recalculation must use the TxScheduled signing payload encoding, while non-scheduled transaction variants continue using their normal encoded_2718 hash path; AsyncExec has an analogous dedicated signing-payload hash path.
suggested_falsification_tests: Hash Scheduled txs before/after runtime metadata; hash should remain signing-payload based.
summary: Scheduled transaction hashing uses signing payloads in the dependency.
test_or_falsification_candidate: Scheduled tx hash should exclude runtime metadata fields.
title: Scheduled transaction hashes use signing payloads
